evidence_quotes: [REAL] Where do you most often buy personal care products? Pick your top 3: A: ['quick_commerce','amazon','pharmacy']

[REAL] Do you use quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto, Swiggy Instamart) for personal care? If yes, does the limited selection change what you buy? A: Yes; would buy a close substitute from limited selection

[SYNTHETIC] You're in a rush and ordering body wash on Zepto/Blinkit for 10-minute delivery. Your usual brand isn't available... How do you choose? A: Pick the one closest to your usual brand's description (e.g., moisturizing, gel-based).
nodes.predictive_value: When targeting this consumer, ensuring product placement and clear benefit descriptors (e.g., 'For Dry Skin') on quick commerce apps is crucial for capturing urgent, substitution-driven purchases.

nodes.label: dry-skin-moisturization-seeker
nodes.description: Has dry skin and rough texture, making moisturization and skin appearance improvement a key desired benefit from a body wash.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What specific skin concerns, if any, do you have? Select all that apply. A: Rough_texture,dryness
[REAL] What is your ideal post-wash skin feeling? A: Moisturized
[REAL] Could you share what specific benefits you would want from this body wash, beyond the packaging and price? A: Should moisturize dry skin and improve skin appearance
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash claim centered on '24-hour moisturization' or 'repairing rough, dry skin', this person would be highly likely to engage with the product details.

nodes.label: ocean-fragrance-gravitation
nodes.description: Strongly gravitates towards ocean/aquatic fragrance families and considers it a key component of their 'perfect' body wash.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What fragrance families do you naturally gravitate toward? Pick your top 2: A: Ocean_aquatic,woody_musky
[REAL] Do you recall if it was more of an oceanic or musky scent, given your preference for those families? A: Ocean
[REAL] If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch, describe it. Think about fragrance, texture, benefit, packaging, price — anything. A: Fragrance: ocean.
nodes.predictive_value: A product sample or ad featuring a clear 'ocean breeze' or 'fresh aquatic' scent description would have a significantly higher chance of capturing their initial interest.

nodes.label: premium-for-clinical-or-indian-made-only
nodes.description: Unwilling to pay a premium for ethical claims (sustainable, cruelty-free, Ayurvedic) but willing to pay 10-20% more for clinical-grade, lab-tested formulations or 'Made in India' products.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? Rate each separately: A: {'Sustainable/eco-friendly packaging':'0','Ayurvedic or traditional ingredients':'0','Clinical-grade, lab-tested formulation':'10_20','Cruelty-free, not tested on animals':'0','Made in India':'10_20'}
[SYNTHETIC] What if your body wash came with a 'Pay ₹199 now, get ₹100 cashback as store credit for next purchase.'... Do you: A: Buy from the cashback store if I plan to shop there again.
nodes.predictive_value: When pricing a new body wash with clinical ingredients, a 10-20% premium over their 300 INR anchor would be acceptable, but a similar premium for 'eco-friendly' packaging would be rejected.

nodes.label: moderately-comfortable-with-novelty-rating-5
nodes.description: Rates themselves as moderately comfortable (5 out of a likely 10-point scale) trying new brands, and has tried new products when prompted by circumstance (e.g., out of stock).
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How comfortable are you trying new brands or products you haven't used before? A: 5
[REAL] Describe the last time you tried a completely new body wash brand. What triggered the switch? Were you satisfied? A: Last tried a new brand because my old one was out of stock. Was satisfied.
[SYNTHETIC] You've been using a body wash brand for 6 months and feel neutral about it... You spot a new launch... Do you: A: Finish your current bottle first, then switch.
nodes.predictive_value: This person is not an early adopter but is open to considered experimentation, especially if their usual option is unavailable or a new product clearly addresses a known gap.

nodes.label: dislikes-sticky-residue-preference
nodes.description: Specifically mentions 'no stickiness left on skin after wash' as a desired improvement, indicating a strong aversion to residue.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Is there anything about the texture or how it feels on your skin that you wish could be improved? A: Three key things: (1) granular texture in the liquid, (2) cooling sensation during use, (3) no stickiness left on skin after wash
[REAL] What is your ideal post-wash skin feeling? A: Moisturized
nodes.predictive_value: When evaluating a product, claims of 'clean rinse' or 'non-sticky feel' would be persuasive differentiators, potentially overriding other positive attributes if stickiness is suspected.

nodes.label: india-specific-formulation-high-interest
nodes.description: Expresses very high interest (rating 5) in a body wash specifically formulated for Indian skin types and hard water conditions.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Would you be interested in a body wash specifically formulated for Indian skin types and hard water conditions? A: {'rating':5,'explanation':''}
[REAL] What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: A: ['india_specific','derm_recommended','ph_balanced']
nodes.predictive_value: A product concept or ad leading with 'Formulated for Indian Hard Water' or 'Made for Indian Skin' would immediately capture their attention and prompt further research.

nodes.label: prefers-personal-scent-over-others-opinion
nodes.description: When choosing a scent, focuses more on what they love personally rather than what they think others will find appealing.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When you're getting ready for the day, do you find yourself more focused on choosing a scent that you love personally, or one that you think others will find appealing? A: One that I love
[REAL] How much do you care about how you smell to others throughout the day? Is grooming more about feeling good yourself, or about how others perceive you, or both? A: Both
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing messages emphasizing 'scent you'll love' or 'personal indulgence' will resonate more than those focusing on 'being attractive to others'.

nodes.label: browse-online-when-runs-out
nodes.description: When their current body wash runs out, their typical next step is to browse online for options, rather than automatically repurchasing the same one.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When your current body wash/soap runs out, what is your typical next step? A: Browse_online
[SYNTHETIC] Your regular body wash brand launches a new variant... Do you: A: Read reviews of the new variant first, then decide.
nodes.predictive_value: This person is in a high-consideration state each time they need a new bottle, making them highly susceptible to targeted online ads and search results at that moment.

nodes.label: dermatologist-recommendation-trusted
nodes.description: Trusts and is influenced by dermatologist recommendations, ranking 'derm_recommended' as a top claim that would make them stop and read more.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: A: ['india_specific','derm_recommended','ph_balanced']

[SYNTHETIC] Two influencers recommend different body washes. Influencer A is a skincare expert... Influencer B is a lifestyle creator... Whose recommendation are you more likely to act on? A: More likely to act on the skincare expert's recommendation due to detailed ingredient analysis.
nodes.predictive_value: Advertising featuring a dermatologist's seal or quote would be significantly more effective than one featuring a lifestyle influencer or celebrity.

nodes.label: self-aware-mood-influences-routine
nodes.description: Describes their relationship with bathing/grooming as 'mood_dependent' and is self-aware enough to not let temporary moods override their strategic purchase criteria.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How would you describe your relationship with bathing/grooming? A: Mood_dependent

[BRANCH] My mood might make me notice the ad more when stressed, but I wouldn't click immediately—I'd save it or look up reviews later when I'm calmer. The decision ultimately depends on the product's features... I'm aware of my moods but try not to let them override my usual criteria.
nodes.predictive_value: While mood-targeted ads (e.g., 'calming lavender for stress') might get initial notice, conversion will still depend on the product meeting their core functional requirements upon later review.

nodes.label: prefers-thick-cream-granular-texture
nodes.description: Prefers a thick cream texture in body wash and specifically desires a 'slightly granular' feel for their perfect product.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What texture do you prefer in a body wash? A: Thick_cream
[REAL] If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch, describe it. Think about fragrance, texture, benefit, packaging, price — anything. A: Texture: slightly granular, thick liquid.
[REAL] Is there anything about the texture or how it feels on your skin that you wish could be improved? A: (1) granular texture in the liquid
nodes.predictive_value: Product imagery or descriptions highlighting a 'creamy, luxe texture with gentle exfoliating granules' would strongly align with their ideal and increase purchase intent.

nodes.label: natural-packaging-aesthetic-preference
nodes.description: Prefers packaging that looks 'Natural' in aesthetic, though they state they 'don't care' about packaging for their perfect product, indicating it's a secondary preference.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do you prefer packaging that looks: A: Natural
[REAL] If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch, describe it. ... Packaging: don't care. Price: moderately premium.
nodes.predictive_value: Packaging design with earthy tones, minimalist typography, or natural imagery would be more appealing than overly glamorous or high-tech looking designs, but won't override product attributes.

nodes.label: indian-brand-value-perception
nodes.description: Perceives Indian brands as offering better value for money personally, but has no strong bias, prioritizing price and quality over origin.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How do you feel about Indian personal care brands vs. international brands? Do you have a preference or bias? A: Price and quality matter; no specific preference
[REAL] When considering price and quality, do you find that Indian or international brands tend to offer better value for you personally? A: Indian

[SYNTHETIC] A body wash brand offers two identical products with different labels: one says 'Made in India' priced at ₹249, the other says 'Imported from Korea' priced at ₹449... Which are you more likely to buy? A: Buy the 'Made in India' option at ₹249 for value and alignment with preferences.
nodes.predictive_value: A product marketed as an Indian brand with a competitive price point will have an inherent value advantage in their consideration set, all else being equal.

nodes.label: deal-evaluator-not-deal-driven
nodes.description: Will evaluate deals and discounts but will not be driven to purchase solely by them; the product must still meet core criteria.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] I'd browse the website first to check if the body wash aligns with my preferences. If it has an ocean fragrance and claims to moisturize dry skin, I might use the coupon. Otherwise, I'd skip it—the deal only matters if the product specifically meets my needs.

[SYNTHETIC] You're browsing Nykaa and find your regular body wash at ₹349. There's a combo offer: buy 2 for ₹549 (saving ₹149). You only need one right now. What do you do? A: Buy just one at ₹349 since I only need one now and prefer not to stock extra.
nodes.predictive_value: A discount coupon for a product they are already researching will increase conversion likelihood, but a discount for an unknown product will not trigger an impulse buy.

nodes.label: trusts-amazon-choice-over-nykaa-luxe-tag
nodes.description: Perceives Amazon's discount-based price framing (e.g., 'MRP ₹799, 25% off') as a better deal than Nykaa's aspirational framing (e.g., 'Luxe' tag) at the same final price.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] You see a body wash priced at ₹599 on Nykaa with a 'Luxe' tag. The same product on Amazon is listed at ₹599 but shown as 'MRP ₹799, 25% off.' ... Which listing feels like a better deal to you? A: Amazon listing with 'MRP ₹799, 25% off' feels like a better deal
nodes.predictive_value: Price promotions framed as a percentage discount off a higher MRP will be more effective on this consumer than luxury or premium branding at the same price point.

nodes.label: skeptical-of-greenwashing-requires-proof
nodes.description: Expresses skepticism towards 'natural' claims and would research a brand accused of greenwashing before making a decision, rather than reacting immediately.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How important is it to you that a product is 'natural' or 'chemical-free'? And what does 'natural' mean to you in your own words? A: {'rating':3,'explanation':'Made from majority of natural products'}

[SYNTHETIC] Imagine you see a body wash brand you love being criticized in a viral Instagram reel for greenwashing. The brand hasn't responded yet. What's your immediate reaction? A: Research the claim myself before deciding.
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing that leans heavily on vague 'natural' or 'clean' claims without substantiation (like certifications) will be met with skepticism and may prompt them to research competing claims.

nodes.label: shared-household-decision-maker
nodes.description: In their household, the body wash is shared with others, but they are the sole decider of what brand/variant to purchase.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do other household members use the same body wash as you? Who decides what to buy? A: Yes, shared. I decide what to buy.
nodes.predictive_value: Their purchase decision considers the shared use, but they have full autonomy. Messaging can focus on the individual's experience ('treat yourself') without needing to appeal to a family.

nodes.label: prefers-mid-range-value-over-extremes
nodes.description: When allocating a budget, prefers to buy two mid-range body washes rather than one premium or three budget options, optimizing for consistent quality and value over time.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Imagine you have ₹500 to spend on body wash for the next 3 months. How would you allocate it? A: Buy two mid-range body washes at ₹250 each to last the full 3 months.
[SYNTHETIC] Rank these body wash brands from 'most premium' to 'least premium' based purely on your gut perception... A: Forest Essentials, The Body Shop, Dove, mCaffeine, Fiama, Nivea, Lux.
nodes.predictive_value: Product portfolios with strong options in the 250-350 INR 'mid-range' segment (like Dove, Nivea) are perfectly positioned for this consumer's default purchasing strategy.

nodes.label: requires-sample-or-trial-before-expensive-commitment
nodes.description: For premium or unknown brands, prefers to try a sample, sachet, or small size before committing to a full-price, full-size purchase.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] A body wash brand offers two purchase options: (A) Full-size 250ml bottle for ₹349 with no returns, (B) A 50ml trial for ₹99... Which option do you choose? A: Choose the 50ml trial for ₹99 with credit toward full size.
[SYNTHETIC] You discover that a body wash you've been curious about is available as a ₹10 sachet at your local kirana store... Would you: A: Buy the sachet to try — perfect low-risk trial.
nodes.predictive_value: Offering affordable trial sizes or sachets is an effective strategy to overcome their risk aversion and introduce them to a new, higher-priced product.

nodes.label: routine-follows-fixed-order
nodes.description: Follows a strict, fixed order in their shower routine (shampoo, body wash, facewash) and rinses completely between each product.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Walk me through your typical bathing routine step by step. What products do you use and in what order? A: Shampoo, Body wash,Facewash
[REAL] Could you share the order in which you use these products during your shower? A: Same order
[REAL] Do you typically rinse off completely between each product, or do you apply them one after the other without rinsing? A: Rinse off between each product
nodes.predictive_value: Products that require a specific order of use or are meant to be left on (like in-shower lotions) may not fit well into their established, efficient routine.

nodes.label: P10_T015
nodes.identity_summary: P10_T015 is a strategic, self-aware student who makes fragrance-driven, value-conscious purchase decisions. They extensively research online reviews before buying, prioritize oceanic scents and moisturizing benefits for dry skin, and prefer Indian brands for better value. While open to trying new products, they distrust influencer marketing and rely on personal connections and platform ratings for validation.

nodes.label: fragrance-is-primary-decision-driver
nodes.description: Ranks fragrance as the #1 factor when buying personal care products, above price and ingredients.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q11: When buying any personal care product, rank these factors from most to least important: A: ["fragrance","price","ingredients","reviews","packaging","brand","recommendation","expert"]
[REAL] Q48: How would you categorize body washes in your mind? What makes one different from another? A: Primarily differentiated by smell
[REAL] Q50: If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch, describe it. Think about fragrance, texture, benefit, packaging, price — anything. A: Fragrance: ocean.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash concept, if the fragrance (especially oceanic) isn't prominently featured or appealing, they're unlikely to engage further.

nodes.label: price-value-balancer-not-extremist
nodes.description: Considers price important but not absolute; willing to pay more for proven benefits like clinical testing, but prefers mid-range options that balance quality and cost.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q11: ...rank these factors: ["fragrance","price","ingredients"]
[REAL] Q21: How much do you typically spend on a single body wash purchase? A: 200_350
[SYNTHETIC] Q68: Imagine you have ₹500 to spend on body wash for the next 3 months... A: Buy two mid-range body washes at ₹250 each for full coverage.
nodes.predictive_value: When presented with a premium product, they'll need clear justification (clinical proof, superior ingredients) for the higher price vs. their usual mid-range options.

nodes.label: online-review-researcher-before-purchase
nodes.description: Almost always browses online and examines review count, star ratings, and price before making a purchase decision.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q19: When your current body wash/soap runs out, what is your typical next step? A: Browse_online
[REAL] Q25: When shopping online for personal care, what do you look at first? Pick your top 3: A: ["review_count","price","star_rating"]
[REAL] Q58: Are there specific review platforms or trusted sources you rely on for these ratings when deciding on a new product? A: Relies on ratings from the purchase platform itself
nodes.predictive_value: Any product launch must have strong, authentic-looking reviews (especially on Amazon/Nykaa) early on to capture their attention and trust.

nodes.label: low-brand-loyalty-high-trial-readiness
nodes.description: Explicitly states low loyalty to personal care brands and is willing to switch easily for better fragrance, to solve skin problems, or based on recommendations.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q18: How loyal are you to personal care brands? A: Low_loyalty
[REAL] Q22: What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: A: ["better_smell","skin_problem","influencer"]
[REAL] Q23: Describe the last time you tried a completely new body wash brand... Was satisfied.
nodes.predictive_value: They are a prime target for new brand launches and competitive switching campaigns, as they aren't entrenched in any brand relationship.

nodes.label: distrusts-paid-influencer-content
nodes.description: Actively distrusts social media influencer recommendations due to perceived paid reviews, preferring platform reviews or personal connections.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q56: Who do you follow or trust in the personal care space on social media? Name specific ones and what makes you trust them. A: None
[REAL] Q60: Is there something specific about social media platforms that makes you hesitant to trust them for product recommendations? A: Paid reviews
[SYNTHETIC] Q272: If you had to allocate 100 points across these factors... What social media influencers say (5)
nodes.predictive_value: Influencer-led marketing campaigns will have low credibility with them unless paired with strong platform reviews or personal endorsements.

nodes.label: trusts-personal-recommendations-over-ads
nodes.description: Values recommendations from friends and family much more than advertising or influencer content, seeing them as more authentic and trustworthy.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] Q61: Rank these five purchase triggers... A: 1) Seeing it in my best friend's bathroom – personal connections make me curious and trusting.
[SYNTHETIC] Q111: You have 10 'trust points' to allocate... A: Mother's recommendation: 3
[SYNTHETIC] Q248: Allocate 10 points across these factors... A: A close friend swears by it: 4
nodes.predictive_value: Word-of-mouth and peer referral programs would be highly effective in driving trial with this consumer.

nodes.label: prefers-indian-brand-value-proposition
nodes.description: Believes Indian brands offer better value for money compared to international ones, and is willing to pay a premium for 'Made in India' products.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q15: When considering price and quality, do you find that Indian or international brands tend to offer better value for you personally? A: Indian
[REAL] Q17: What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? ..."Made in India":"10_20"
[SYNTHETIC] Q84: A body wash brand offers two identical products... Which are you more likely to buy? A: Buy the 'Made in India' option at ₹249.
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing that emphasizes Indian formulation, sourcing, or value-for-money will resonate more than international prestige.

nodes.label: oceanic-scent-enthusiast
nodes.description: Strongly gravitates toward ocean/aquatic fragrance families and specifically desires this scent in their ideal body wash.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q35: What fragrance families do you naturally gravitate toward? Pick your top 2: A: Ocean_aquatic,woody_musky
[REAL] Q37: Do you recall if it was more of an oceanic or musky scent, given your preference for those families? A: Ocean
[REAL] Q50: If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch... A: Fragrance: ocean.
nodes.predictive_value: Body wash concepts featuring fresh, aquatic, or oceanic fragrance notes will have a significantly higher chance of capturing their initial interest.

nodes.label: moisturizing-benefit-seeker-for-dry-skin
nodes.description: Has dry skin concerns and prioritizes body washes that leave skin feeling moisturized and improve skin appearance.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q33: What specific skin concerns, if any, do you have? Select all that apply. A: Rough_texture,dryness
[REAL] Q41: What is your ideal post-wash skin feeling? A: Moisturized
[REAL] Q51: Could you share what specific benefits you would want from this body wash... A: Should moisturize dry skin and improve skin appearance
nodes.predictive_value: Product claims around intense moisturization, hydration, and improving dry/rough skin texture will be key purchase drivers.

nodes.label: strategic-non-impulsive-shopper
nodes.description: Describes self as 'strategic' and 'self-aware'; this manifests in avoiding impulse buys, letting coupons expire if not needed, and thorough research before purchasing.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q6: How would you describe your personality in 3-5 words? A: Strategic. Intense. Self-aware
[BRANCH] Q64: A body wash brand you've never tried sends you a ₹150 discount coupon valid for 48 hours... A: I'd let the coupon expire. My current body wash works fine...
[SYNTHETIC] Q154: You're choosing between two body washes at a store. Your mobile data is slow... A: Leave without buying and research at home.
nodes.predictive_value: Time-limited flash sales or pressure tactics will be less effective; they need time and information to make a considered decision.

nodes.label: quick-commerce-substitution-acceptor
nodes.description: Frequently uses quick commerce apps for personal care purchases and is willing to buy a close substitute when preferred brand is unavailable due to limited selection.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q20: Where do you most often buy personal care products? Pick your top 3: A: ["quick_commerce","amazon","pharmacy"]

[REAL] Q26: Do you use quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto, Swiggy Instamart) for personal care? If yes, does the limited selection change what you buy? A: Yes; would buy a close substitute from limited selection
[SYNTHETIC] Q331: ...You need body wash today. Would you: A: pick from Zepto's limited range for instant delivery.
nodes.predictive_value: Ensuring product availability and prominent placement on quick commerce apps is crucial for capturing their urgent/impromptu purchases.

nodes.label: ingredient-label-reader-for-key-components
nodes.description: Checks ingredient lists, focusing on recognizing key components like SLS (sulfate-free) and looking for certifications like pH-balanced.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q13: Which certifications or labels influence your purchase? Select all that apply. A: Ph_balanced,sulfate_free
[SYNTHETIC] Q115: What if a body wash listed 'Aqua, Sodium Laureth Sulfate...'? A: Recognize a few key ones like SLS.
[SYNTHETIC] Q117: A colleague at work mentions they switched to a sulfate-free body wash... A: Look for sulfate-free options and compare multiple brands.
nodes.predictive_value: Product packaging and online listings should clearly highlight key ingredient benefits (sulfate-free, pH-balanced) to pass their initial scrutiny.

nodes.label: clinical-lab-testing-premium-payer
nodes.description: Willing to pay a 10-20% premium specifically for clinical-grade, lab-tested formulations, indicating trust in scientific validation.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q17: What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? ..."Clinical-grade, lab-tested formulation":"10_20"
[REAL] Q52: What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: A: ["india_specific","derm_recommended","ph_balanced"]
[SYNTHETIC] Q98: What if your regular ₹299 body wash launched a 'dermatologist-recommended' version at ₹399... A: I'd need to see the clinical study results first.
nodes.predictive_value: Claims of dermatologist recommendation, clinical testing, or lab efficacy will justify a higher price point and increase their consideration.

nodes.label: values-in-store-testing-before-online-purchase
nodes.description: Prefers to smell and test products in physical stores when possible, allocating significant pre-purchase effort to this activity.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q55: Where do you typically discover new personal care products? Pick your top 3: A: ["tv","in_store","quick_commerce"]
[SYNTHETIC] Q149: You have 100 points to allocate across these activities... A: Smelling/testing it in a store: 30
[SYNTHETIC] Q237: Imagine two scenarios for trying a new body wash... A: Scenario A — testing in person makes me more likely to buy.
nodes.predictive_value: In-store sampling, testers, and discoverability in pharmacies/retail stores are important for driving initial trial and eventual online purchase.

nodes.label: responsive-to-india-specific-formulation-claims
nodes.description: Highly interested (rating 5/5) in body washes specifically formulated for Indian skin types and hard water conditions.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q54: Would you be interested in a body wash specifically formulated for Indian skin types and hard water conditions? A: {"rating":5,"explanation":""}
[REAL] Q52: What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: A: ["india_specific","derm_recommended","ph_balanced"]

[SYNTHETIC] Q229: You see a body wash on Amazon India with the description: 'Made with an ancient tribal recipe from Northeast India...' Would you try it... A: Would try it; primary driver is the India-specific and derm-recommended feel.
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing that highlights adaptation to Indian climate, water quality, or skin types will be a powerful stopping and consideration trigger.

nodes.label: prefers-thick-cream-granular-texture
nodes.description: Explicitly prefers a thick cream texture in body wash, and in their ideal product, desires a slightly granular feel within that thick liquid.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q40: What texture do you prefer in a body wash? A: Thick_cream
[REAL] Q46: Is there anything about the texture or how it feels on your skin that you wish could be improved? A: Three key things: (1) granular texture in the liquid...
[REAL] Q50: If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch... A: Texture: slightly granular, thick liquid.
nodes.predictive_value: Product concepts featuring a rich, creamy, or gently exfoliating granular texture will align perfectly with their sensory preferences.

nodes.label: avoids-subscription-and-commitment-models
nodes.description: Prefers one-time purchases over subscriptions to maintain flexibility and freedom to switch brands easily.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Q77: A new D2C body wash brand offers you two options... Which do you choose? A: Choose the one-time purchase at ₹349 to avoid subscription commitment.
[SYNTHETIC] Q198: You're browsing Amazon India and see your regular body wash has a 'Subscribe & Save' option... A: Don't subscribe — I like the freedom to switch brands anytime.

[SYNTHETIC] Q326: Imagine you could set up automatic body wash reordering... Which system would you prefer? A: Amazon Subscribe & Save for discount and automation. (Note: This shows a preference for discount but the earlier answers indicate aversion to commitment; this might be a contradiction. We'll keep confidence at 0.8 due to some conflicting signals.)
nodes.predictive_value: Subscription-based purchase models will have low uptake; they prefer the control of discrete purchases.

nodes.label: mood-aware-but-not-driven-purchaser
nodes.description: Acknowledges that grooming is mood-dependent and stressful moods may make them notice relevant ads, but the final decision remains grounded in strategic criteria.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q32: How would you describe your relationship with bathing/grooming? A: Mood_dependent

[BRANCH] Q65: You've had a long, stressful day. While scrolling... you see an ad for a new body wash with a calming lavender scent... A: My mood might make me notice the ad more when stressed, but I wouldn't click immediately—I'd save it or look up reviews later when I'm calmer.
nodes.predictive_value: Mood-targeted advertising (e.g., 'de-stress', 'energize') can increase top-of-mind awareness, but must be followed by substantive product proof points to convert.

nodes.label: trusts-everyday-people-ads-over-luxury-fantasies
nodes.description: Finds advertising featuring everyday people in realistic settings more trustworthy and relatable than luxurious, aspirational spa-like visuals.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] Q63: Two body wash ads play on TV. Ad 1: A model in a luxurious spa setting... Ad 2: A collage of everyday people in their bathrooms... A: Ad 2 with everyday people is more my style. It shows how the body wash fits into my actual routine...
[REAL] Q57: When you see personal care products on TV or in-store, what kind of endorsements or signs make you feel confident about trying them? A: Rating/Brand
nodes.predictive_value: Advertising creative should focus on authenticity, practical routine integration, and social proof (ratings) rather than pure luxury or escapism.

nodes.label: trial-size-tester-for-new-brands
nodes.description: Prefers to try new brands through small trial sizes, sachets, or samples before committing to a full-size purchase, mitigating risk.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] Q61: Rank these five purchase triggers... A: 2) Free sample sachets – experiencing the smell and texture firsthand is key.
[SYNTHETIC] Q212: You had a bad experience with a new body wash last month... Do you: A: Only try if they offer a 50ml trial size
[SYNTHETIC] Q217: A body wash brand offers two purchase options... A: Choose the 50ml trial for ₹99 with credit toward full size.
nodes.predictive_value: Offering affordable trial sizes or free samples is an effective strategy to drive first-time trial and reduce their perceived risk.

nodes.label: skeptical-of-greenwashing-and-gimmicky-claims
nodes.description: Questions 'natural' claims, is skeptical of sustainability premiums without proof, and dismisses novel features (color-changing, AI) as gimmicks unless backed by real benefit.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Q102: ...How much of the ₹80 difference do you feel is justified? A: About ₹30-40 — I value sustainability but not enough to pay full difference.

[SYNTHETIC] Q151: During a conversation, a friend mentions that most 'natural' body washes in India aren't truly natural... A: D) Test this claim by comparing ingredient lists of 'natural' vs regular body washes.
[SYNTHETIC] Q218: ...Would you buy it primarily for: A: I wouldn't buy it — sounds like a gimmick.
nodes.predictive_value: Sustainability or 'clean beauty' claims must be substantiated and not command a significant price premium. Focus on tangible benefits over novelty.

nodes.label: platform-loyalty-over-brand-loyalty
nodes.description: Demonstrates strong habitual use of specific purchase channels (Amazon, quick commerce) and trusts their review systems more than brand-specific channels.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q20: Where do you most often buy personal care products? Pick your top 3: A: ["quick_commerce","amazon","pharmacy"]
[REAL] Q58: Relies on ratings from the purchase platform itself
[SYNTHETIC] Q333: You read a detailed body wash review on a blog, and the blogger provides both an Amazon link and a direct brand website link... A: Click Amazon link.
nodes.predictive_value: Brands should prioritize strong presence and review management on Amazon, Nykaa, and quick commerce apps over driving traffic to their own D2C sites.

nodes.label: comparison-shopper-across-channels
nodes.description: Actively compares prices across online platforms and is willing to visit physical stores to save money, indicating price consciousness within their considered set.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Q306: ...Would you drive to DMart to save ₹50, or buy it on Nykaa right now? A: Drive to DMart to save ₹50.
[SYNTHETIC] Q351: During a Nykaa sale, you add a body wash to your cart but get distracted... Do you: A: check if it's cheaper elsewhere now
[SYNTHETIC] Q79: Allocate 100 points across these five factors... A: Price/deal available right now: 40
nodes.predictive_value: Promotions and pricing must be competitive across major channels (Amazon, Nykaa, quick commerce, retail) to prevent them from shopping elsewhere.

nodes.label: natural-packaging-aesthetic-preference
nodes.description: Prefers packaging that looks natural, and this visual aesthetic is a key attention-grabber when browsing online.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q42: Do you prefer packaging that looks: A: Natural

[BRANCH] Q62: When researching a new body wash online before purchase, what typically captures your attention first... A: The product images and packaging visuals grab my attention first—if it looks natural and appealing...
nodes.predictive_value: Product packaging and primary online images should feature a natural, earthy, or clean aesthetic to draw their initial click and interest.

nodes.label: relies-on-high-review-volume-for-trust
nodes.description: Trusts products with a high number of reviews (thousands) more than those with a very high rating but few reviews, seeking the safety of crowd validation.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Q95: You're comparing body washes online and see these ratings: Brand A: 4.2 stars, 15,000 reviews, ₹279. Brand B: 4.8 stars, 120 reviews, ₹329. Which do you trust more and buy? A: Brand A: 4.2 stars with 15,000 reviews at ₹279.

[SYNTHETIC] Q167: You're choosing between two body washes on Nykaa: Brand A has a 4.5-star rating with 12,000 reviews. Brand B has a 4.8-star rating with only 85 reviews... A: Brand A — more reviews mean more reliable ratings.
[REAL] Q25: ...what do you look at first? Pick your top 3: A: ["review_count","price","star_rating"]
nodes.predictive_value: New products need strategies to generate a critical mass of reviews quickly to appear trustworthy in their online searches.

nodes.label: P10_T016
nodes.identity_summary: P10_T016 is a strategic, self-aware student whose body wash decisions are driven by a strong sensory preference for ocean/aquatic fragrances and a functional need for moisturizing benefits to address dry skin. They exhibit low brand loyalty, actively comparing prices and reading online reviews before purchase, and place higher trust in recommendations from friends and dermatologists than in advertising or social media influencers. While open to trying new products, their research-heavy approach and focus on value for money make them a discerning consumer who is willing to pay a premium only for proven, clinically-tested formulations tailored to Indian conditions.

nodes.label: fragrance-driven-selection
nodes.description: Prioritizes fragrance, especially ocean/aquatic scents, over other factors when selecting a body wash.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q11: ...rank these factors from most to least important: A: ['fragrance','price','ingredients','reviews','packaging','brand','recommendation','expert']
[REAL] Q24: What was it about the new body wash that made you feel satisfied after trying it? A: Smell
[REAL] Q36: Name a specific product whose fragrance you loved. ... A: A Nivea product; loved the fragrance.
[REAL] Q48: How would you categorize body washes in your mind? A: Primarily differentiated by smell
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash concept with an ocean/aquatic fragrance, this person would be highly inclined to explore it further, even if other attributes are unknown.

nodes.label: price-comparison-habit
nodes.description: Habitually compares prices across channels and platforms to find the best deal before purchase.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q19: When your current body wash/soap runs out, what is your typical next step? A: Browse_online
[REAL] Q25: When shopping online for personal care, what do you look at first? A: ['review_count','price','star_rating']
[SYNTHETIC] Q72: ...What's your most likely reaction? A: Switch to the ₹249 alternative without much thought.
[SYNTHETIC] Q88: ...How does this make you feel, and what do you do next time? A: Indifferent — prices change, it's normal.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad with a price comparison or a 'lowest price guaranteed' claim, this person would be more likely to click through to verify the deal.

nodes.label: online-review-reliant
nodes.description: Heavily relies on online reviews and star ratings from the purchase platform itself as a primary source of validation.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q25: When shopping online for personal care, what do you look at first? A: ['review_count','price','star_rating']
[REAL] Q58: Are there specific review platforms or trusted sources you rely on for these ratings when deciding on a new product? A: Relies on ratings from the purchase platform itself

[SYNTHETIC] Q95: You're comparing body washes online and see these ratings: Brand A: 4.2 stars, 15,000 reviews, ₹279. Brand B: 4.8 stars, 120 reviews, ₹329. Which do you trust more and buy? A: Brand A: 4.2 stars, 15,000 reviews, ₹279
[SYNTHETIC] Q106: ...Which one do you click on first? A: Click on the one with 2,400 ratings and 4.1 average first
nodes.predictive_value: When a product page lacks a substantial number of reviews or has a low star rating, this person would be highly unlikely to proceed with the purchase without further investigation.

nodes.label: low-brand-loyalty
nodes.description: Exhibits low loyalty to personal care brands and is willing to switch for better smell, to solve a skin problem, or due to an influencer's recommendation.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q18: How loyal are you to personal care brands? A: Low_loyalty
[REAL] Q22: What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: A: ['better_smell','skin_problem','influencer']
[REAL] Q23: ...Last tried a new brand because my old one was out of stock.
[SYNTHETIC] Q178: Think about the last 3 body washes you purchased. ... A: C) Different brands each time.
nodes.predictive_value: When a competing brand offers a compelling new variant with an appealing fragrance or skin benefit, this person would readily consider switching, regardless of their current brand.

nodes.label: values-dermatologist-recommendations
nodes.description: Stops to read more about products with dermatologist recommendations and values clinical, lab-tested claims, willing to pay a premium for them.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q52: What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? A: ['india_specific','derm_recommended','ph_balanced']
[REAL] Q17: What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? ... A: {'Clinical-grade, lab-tested formulation':'10_20'}
[BRANCH] Q63: ...The ad style is secondary—what matters is whether the body wash delivers on claims, especially for my skin concerns like dryness.
[SYNTHETIC] Q98: ...Would you upgrade? A: C) I'd need to see the clinical study results first
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad featuring a dermatologist or a 'clinically tested' badge, this person would be more likely to pause and consider the product, especially if it addresses dryness.

nodes.label: friend-recommendation-trust
nodes.description: Trusts personal recommendations from friends and family more than influencer endorsements, ads, or even some expert opinions.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] Q61: Rank these five purchase triggers from most to least likely... A: I'd go: 1) Seeing it in my best friend's bathroom – personal connections make me curious and trusting.
[SYNTHETIC] Q107: ...You can only buy one this month. Which do you choose? A: Choose the body wash my close friend loves
[SYNTHETIC] Q136: ...at what stage would you consider buying? A: After someone I personally know recommends it, as personal trust matters most.
[SYNTHETIC] Q272: If you had to allocate 100 points across these factors... A: What friends/family recommend (10), What social media influencers say (5), ...
nodes.predictive_value: When a friend shares or tags them in a post about a body wash, this person is highly likely to investigate the product, more so than if they saw a paid influencer ad.

nodes.label: ingredient-aware-but-not-expert
nodes.description: Checks ingredient lists for specific, recognized terms (like pH-balanced, sulfate-free, SLS) but does not conduct deep, independent research on every ingredient.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q13: Which certifications or labels influence your purchase? Select all that apply. A: Ph_balanced,sulfate_free
[SYNTHETIC] Q110: You notice your body wash has 'Paraben-Free' printed on it. What would you most likely do? A: B) Flip the bottle to verify in the ingredient list
[SYNTHETIC] Q115: What if a body wash listed 'Aqua, Sodium Laureth Sulfate, Cocamidopropyl Betaine...' on the back label? Would you... A: B) recognize a few key ones like SLS
[SYNTHETIC] Q131: ...Would you... A: Continue using it since it hasn't caused issues but stay open to alternatives.
nodes.predictive_value: When a product's front-of-pack highlights 'pH-balanced' or 'Sulfate-Free', this person will likely check the ingredient list for validation, and if found, it builds trust.

nodes.label: quick-commerce-adopter-for-immediacy
nodes.description: Uses quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto) for urgent replenishment, accepting a limited selection or a close substitute for the sake of immediate delivery.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q20: Where do you most often buy personal care products? Pick your top 3: A: ['quick_commerce','amazon','pharmacy']

[REAL] Q26: Do you use quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto, Swiggy Instamart) for personal care? If yes, does the limited selection change what you buy? A: Yes; would buy a close substitute from limited selection
[SYNTHETIC] Q69: ...What do you do? A: Order from Blinkit at ₹299 for immediate delivery since I'm almost out.
[SYNTHETIC] Q331: ...Would you: A: a) pick from Zepto's limited range for instant delivery.
nodes.predictive_value: When prompted by a 'running low' notification or in an urgent need scenario, this person will favor a quick commerce app over waiting for a better-priced online delivery.

nodes.label: skin-concern-aware-moisturization-seeker
nodes.description: Actively seeks body washes that address specific skin concerns of dryness and rough texture, with a strong preference for a moisturized post-wash feel.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q33: What specific skin concerns, if any, do you have? Select all that apply. A: Rough_texture,dryness
[REAL] Q41: What is your ideal post-wash skin feeling? A: Moisturized
[REAL] Q51: Could you share what specific benefits you would want from this body wash... A: Should moisturize dry skin and improve skin appearance
[BRANCH] Q63: ...what matters is whether the body wash delivers on claims, especially for my skin concerns like dryness.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash concept that prominently features '24-hour moisturization' or 'skin barrier repair' claims, this person would be highly motivated to read the details.

nodes.label: texture-and-sensation-seeker
nodes.description: Desires specific physical attributes in a body wash: a thick, slightly granular cream texture that provides a cooling sensation during use and leaves no stickiness.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q40: What texture do you prefer in a body wash? A: Thick_cream

[REAL] Q46: Is there anything about the texture or how it feels on your skin that you wish could be improved? A: Three key things: (1) granular texture in the liquid, (2) cooling sensation during use, (3) no stickiness left on skin after wash
[REAL] Q50: If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch, describe it... A: Texture: slightly granular, thick liquid.
[SYNTHETIC] Q226: ...Which do you pick? A: Pick the oil-to-foam formula with the earthy vetiver scent.
nodes.predictive_value: When a product description or ad video highlights a 'granular', 'cooling', or 'non-sticky' texture, this person would be more inclined to consider a trial purchase.

nodes.label: strategic-research-before-purchase
nodes.description: Takes a deliberate, time-allocated approach to research, comparing prices, reading reviews, and checking ingredients before making a purchase decision.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q6: How would you describe your personality in 3-5 words? A: Strategic. Intense. Self-aware
[BRANCH] Q64: ...I'd browse the website first to check if the body wash aligns with my preferences.
[SYNTHETIC] Q118: Imagine you have 30 minutes before buying a body wash online. How would you split that time? A: 15 min comparing prices, 10 min reading text reviews, 5 min reading ingredient lists.
[SYNTHETIC] Q154: ...Your mobile data is slow and pages are loading poorly. Do you... A: B. Decide based on packaging info alone if data is slow.
nodes.predictive_value: When presented with a new product, this person will not make an impulse purchase; they will leave the site/app to conduct cross-platform research, lengthening the consideration phase.

nodes.label: ad-skeptic-paid-review-averse
nodes.description: Highly skeptical of social media ads and influencer endorsements, perceiving them as paid and inauthentic, and rarely acts on them directly.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q59: Have you ever bought a personal care product because you saw it on social media? Describe what happened. A: No
[REAL] Q60: Is there something specific about social media platforms that makes you hesitant to trust them for product recommendations? A: Paid reviews
[REAL] Q57: When you see personal care products on TV or in-store, what kind of endorsements or signs make you feel confident about trying them? A: Rating/Brand
[SYNTHETIC] Q119: ...Do you... A: Check another platform for more authentic reviews to verify the overly positive ones.
nodes.predictive_value: When served a typical influencer ad on Instagram, this person is more likely to scroll past or screenshot for later verification than to click the 'Shop Now' link immediately.

nodes.label: moderate-risk-taker-prefers-samples
nodes.description: Moderately comfortable trying new brands but prefers to mitigate risk through free samples, trial sizes, or money-back guarantees before committing to a full-size purchase.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q7: How comfortable are you trying new brands or products you haven't used before? A: 5
[BRANCH] Q61: ...2) Free sample sachets – experiencing the smell and texture firsthand is key.
[SYNTHETIC] Q212: ...Do you: A) Only try if they offer a 50ml trial size
[SYNTHETIC] Q217: A body wash brand offers two purchase options... Which option do you choose? A: Choose the 50ml trial for ₹99 with credit toward full size if I like it.
nodes.predictive_value: When a new brand offers a low-cost trial size or a free sample with another order, this person is highly likely to try it, converting to a full-size purchase if satisfied.

nodes.label: India-specific-claims-responsive
nodes.description: Highly interested in and responsive to claims about products being formulated for Indian skin types, hard water conditions, or incorporating traditional Indian ingredients.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q52: What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? A: ['india_specific','derm_recommended','ph_balanced']
[REAL] Q54: Would you be interested in a body wash specifically formulated for Indian skin types and hard water conditions? A: {'rating':5,'explanation':''}
[REAL] Q17: ...Maximum premium for 'Made in India':'10_20'
[SYNTHETIC] Q229: ...Would you try it, and what's the primary driver of your decision? A: I would try it; primary driver is the unique India-specific story and packaging.
nodes.predictive_value: When a body wash ad leads with 'Made for Indian Hard Water' or 'Inspired by Ayurveda', this person will stop to read the details more closely than for a generic international brand ad.

nodes.label: natural-claim-moderate-skeptic
nodes.description: Assigns moderate importance to 'natural' claims, defining it pragmatically as 'made from majority of natural products', but is not swayed by vague marketing and won't pay a premium for it.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q12: How important is it to you that a product is 'natural' or 'chemical-free'? And what does 'natural' mean to you in your own words? A: {'rating':3,'explanation':'Made from majority of natural products'}
[REAL] Q17: ...Maximum premium for 'Ayurvedic or traditional ingredients':'0'
[SYNTHETIC] Q151: ...This makes you want to... A: E. Continue as before since 'natural' claims rate low on my priority.
nodes.predictive_value: When a product heavily markets itself as '100% Natural', this person will be skeptical and look for ingredient list validation, but it won't be a primary purchase driver.

nodes.label: sustainable-packaging-indifferent
nodes.description: Unwilling to pay any premium for sustainable or eco-friendly packaging, indicating it is not a valued attribute in purchase decisions.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q17: What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? A: {'Sustainable/eco-friendly packaging':'0'}
[SYNTHETIC] Q102: ...How much of the ₹80 difference do you feel is justified? A: C) ₹0 — these claims don't influence what I'm willing to pay
[SYNTHETIC] Q146: ...Does this... A: D. I'd compare this cost breakdown with other brands before deciding.
nodes.predictive_value: When a body wash is marketed primarily on its eco-friendly packaging, this person would not be persuaded to choose it over a functionally equivalent, cheaper alternative.

nodes.label: prefers-mid-range-value-pricing
nodes.description: Prefers to spend in the 200-350 INR range for a single body wash, seeking a balance between quality and price, and typically buys mid-tier options.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q21: How much do you typically spend on a single body wash purchase? A: 200_350
[SYNTHETIC] Q68: Imagine you have ₹500 to spend on body wash for the next 3 months. How would you allocate it? A: Buy two mid-range body washes at ₹250 each for consistent quality over 3 months.
[SYNTHETIC] Q86: ...Rank them in order of purchase preference: A: B) ₹299 mid-tier moisturizing, A) ₹149 basic trusted brand, C) ₹499 organic eco-friendly D2C.
[SYNTHETIC] Q192: You have ₹500 to spend on body wash this month. How do you allocate it? A: B) Two bottles of a mid-range brand (₹250 each) — one for daily use, one to try something new.
nodes.predictive_value: When presented with a premium body wash over ₹500, this person would deem it overpriced unless it offers exceptional, proven benefits not found in mid-range options.

nodes.label: self-aware-balances-personal-social-smell
nodes.description: Self-aware about grooming, choosing scents they love personally but also caring about how they smell to others throughout the day.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q6: How would you describe your personality in 3-5 words? A: Strategic. Intense. Self-aware
[REAL] Q8: How much do you care about how you smell to others throughout the day? Is grooming more about feeling good yourself, or about how others perceive you, or both? A: Both
[REAL] Q9: When you're getting ready for the day, do you find yourself more focused on choosing a scent that you love personally, or one that you think others will find appealing? A: One that I love
nodes.predictive_value: When evaluating a fragrance, this person will prioritize their own enjoyment but will also consider its social acceptability, making them receptive to scents described as 'universally appealing' or 'fresh and clean'.

nodes.label: mood-dependent-grooming-engagement
nodes.description: The level of engagement with bathing and grooming routines fluctuates with their mood, which can indirectly affect product choice and experimentation.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q32: How would you describe your relationship with bathing/grooming? A: Mood_dependent
[BRANCH] Q65: ...My mood might make me notice the ad more when stressed, but I wouldn't click immediately—I'd save it or look up reviews later when I'm calmer.
[SYNTHETIC] Q268: Imagine you're on a first date and want to feel extra confident. Would you... A: Use a special/premium body wash you reserve for important occasions.
nodes.predictive_value: During periods of low mood or stress, this person is less likely to try a new product; marketing that taps into self-care or mood enhancement might capture their attention but not immediate action.

nodes.label: discount-attracted-not-decisive
nodes.description: Attracted by discounts and deals, but they will not drive a purchase unless the product itself aligns with core preferences (fragrance, skin benefit).
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] Q64: A body wash brand you've never tried sends you a ₹150 discount coupon... What do you do? A: I'd browse the website first to check if the body wash aligns with my preferences. If it has an ocean fragrance and claims to moisturize dry skin, I might use the coupon. Otherwise, I'd skip it—the deal only matters if the product specifically meets my needs.

[SYNTHETIC] Q92: If you had to choose between these two deals for body wash, which would you pick? Deal A: Buy 1 body wash (250ml) at ₹299 and get a free loofah worth ₹99. Deal B: Buy 1 body wash (250ml) at ₹259, no freebies. A: Deal B: Buy 1 body wash (250ml) at ₹259, no freebies.

[SYNTHETIC] Q96: ...Rank these discount presentations from most to least appealing: A) '₹100 off — now ₹199' B) '33% off' ... A: A) '₹100 off — now ₹199' B) '33% off' E) 'Free shipping (worth ₹50) + ₹50 off' C) 'Buy 2 Get 1 Free' D) '₹100 cashback on Paytm'
nodes.predictive_value: A flash sale email will get them to browse, but they will only convert if the product's core attributes (fragrance, moisturization) are clearly communicated and match their desires.

nodes.label: convenience-driven-format-preference
nodes.description: Prefers body wash over bar soap primarily for convenience and values practical packaging formats like pump bottles for daily use.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q31: Do you currently use body wash, bar soap, or both? What drives that choice? A: Body wash; driven by convenience
[SYNTHETIC] Q260: You're visiting your parents' home for a week. They only have a bar soap in the bathroom. What do you do? A: A. Use the bar soap without complaint.
[SYNTHETIC] Q328: ...Which format do you choose for your daily home use? A: Pump bottle for daily home use.
nodes.predictive_value: When presented with a new, more convenient format (e.g., a one-click foam pump), this person would be interested, but a format that adds steps (like a powder to mix) would be less appealing.

nodes.label: multi-platform-researcher
nodes.description: Does not rely on a single source; cross-checks product information, reviews, and prices across multiple platforms (Amazon, Nykaa, Google) before deciding.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Q114: ...Do you (A) order a familiar brand without further research, (B) pause and open Nykaa/Amazon to read reviews first, or (C) quickly Google the top options before ordering? A: B) pause and open Nykaa/Amazon to read reviews first

[SYNTHETIC] Q119: ...Do you (A) trust them anyway since the rating is high, (B) scroll to find negative/critical reviews specifically, (C) check another platform for more authentic reviews, (D) look for video reviews from real users on YouTube? A: Check another platform for more authentic reviews to verify the overly positive ones.

[SYNTHETIC] Q126: ...Would you (A) order it trusting the bestseller tag, (B) search for the product on Amazon/Nykaa to read reviews before ordering on Zepto, (C) skip it and pick something you already know, (D) check the brand's Instagram for more info? A: Search for the product on Amazon/Nykaa to read reviews before ordering on Zepto.
nodes.predictive_value: If a product has inconsistent information or reviews across platforms, this person will likely delay purchase until they can reconcile the discrepancies or find more consensus.

nodes.label: prefers-visual-packaging-first-impression
nodes.description: Product images and packaging visuals that look natural and appealing are the first hook that captures their attention online.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q42: Do you prefer packaging that looks: A: Natural

[BRANCH] Q62: ...what typically captures your attention first: the product images and packaging visuals, or the written description, ingredients list, and customer reviews? A: The product images and packaging visuals grab my attention first—if it looks natural and appealing, like from my preference in Q42, I'm intrigued.
[SYNTHETIC] Q108: ...What do you look at on the packaging? Rank these: A: B) Front label claims, C) MRP and quantity, D) Brand name, E) Manufacturing/expiry date, A) Ingredient list
nodes.predictive_value: A body wash with minimalist, natural-looking packaging photography will be more effective at stopping their scroll on an e-commerce site than a cluttered or overly flashy design.

nodes.label: cruelty-free-claim-indifferent
nodes.description: Unwilling to pay a premium for cruelty-free claims, indicating animal testing is not a primary factor in their purchase decisions.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q17: What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? A: {'Cruelty-free, not tested on animals':'0'}
[SYNTHETIC] Q191: ...What do you do? A: B) Feel conflicted but continue buying while looking for alternatives.
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing a body wash solely on its 'Leaping Bunny' or 'PETA-approved' certification will not influence this person's choice if the product is otherwise similar to a cheaper alternative.

nodes.label: fragrance-longevity-expectant
nodes.description: Expects a body wash fragrance to last on their skin for about half a day, setting a benchmark for product performance.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q38: How long should a body wash fragrance last on your skin? A: Half_day

[SYNTHETIC] Q265: ...Would you: A) Rush-buy a new fragrant body wash specifically for the event. B) Use your regular body wash and layer with a perfume/deo instead. C) Borrow a fragrant body wash from a friend or family member. A: B. Use my regular body wash and layer with perfume/deo.
nodes.predictive_value: If a body wash is advertised with 'all-day fragrance', this person would be skeptical and look for reviews specifically commenting on scent longevity before believing the claim.

nodes.label: avoids-subscription-commitment
nodes.description: Prefers one-time purchases over subscriptions to maintain flexibility and avoid being locked into a brand or product.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Q77: A new D2C body wash brand offers you two options: Option A: ₹349 one-time purchase. Option B: ₹279/month subscription with auto-delivery every 45 days (cancel anytime). Which do you choose? A: Choose the ₹349 one-time purchase to avoid subscription commitment.

[SYNTHETIC] Q169: Your current body wash brand starts a loyalty program: buy 5 bottles, get the 6th free. But it requires you to register with your phone number and get promotional messages. Do you: A) Skip it — I don't want promotional messages.

[SYNTHETIC] Q198: ...Do you: A) Subscribe — 10% off and one less thing to remember. B) Don't subscribe — I like the freedom to switch brands anytime. nodes.label: P10_T017
nodes.identity_summary: A strategic and self-aware student in Ahmedabad, P10_T017 is a pragmatic and research-driven consumer with low brand loyalty. They prioritize scent (especially oceanic/musky), skin benefits for dryness, and price-value balance. Their purchase decisions are heavily influenced by online reviews, dermatologist endorsements, and clinical claims, but they are skeptical of social media influencers and paid promotions. They actively compare options and will switch brands for better smell, skin improvements, or price advantages, displaying a mood-dependent relationship with grooming.

nodes.label: driven-by-primary-fragrance-experience

[REAL] How would you categorize body washes in your mind? What makes one different from another? A: Primarily differentiated by smell
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash concept, this person would likely prioritize evaluating its fragrance profile over other claims because scent is their primary differentiator and driver of satisfaction.

nodes.label: price-value-balancer-not-bargain-hunter

[REAL] What do you like MOST about your current body wash? (Top 1-2 things) A: Smell and price

nodes.label: online-review-reliant-social-proof-seeker

[REAL] Are there specific review platforms or trusted sources you rely on for these ratings when deciding on a new product? A: Relies on ratings from the purchase platform itself

If you could only view one before deciding, which do you choose? A: Detailed ingredient breakdown with percentages.
nodes.predictive_value: When evaluating a new product online, this person would likely skip past marketing videos and immediately scroll to the customer review section, prioritizing products with a high volume of reviews over those with a slightly higher but less-populated rating.

A: Influencer A, the skincare expert with detailed ingredient analysis.

nodes.label: mood-dependent-grooming-ritualist

nodes.label: avoids-subscription-and-commitment

[SYNTHETIC] Your regular body wash brand starts a loyalty program: buy 5 bottles, get the 6th free. But it requires you to register with your phone number and get promotional messages. Do you: A: Skip it — I don't want promotional messages.

[SYNTHETIC] You're browsing Amazon India and notice your regular body wash brand now has a 'Subscribe & Save' option with 10% off. You've never subscribed to any personal care product before. Do you: A: Don't subscribe — I like the freedom to switch brands anytime.

nodes.label: novelty-curious-but-risk-managed

nodes.label: platform-price-comparison-habituated

nodes.label: P10_T018

Their mood significantly influences their grooming routine and receptiveness to self-care marketing.
